title: The World as a Living Book — Ficino, Hermes, and the Recovery of Divine Order
author: Hermes Trismegistus / Marsilio Ficino

body.text: Ficino and Hermes share a single premise. The universe is a book written by Mind. To read it is an act of devotion. The ordinary person sees things arranged. The Hermetic philosopher sees how they are ordered — a lattice of meaning scored into everything.

For there can be no Religion more true or just, than to know the things that are; and to acknowledge thanks for all things, to him that made them. Be Pious and Religious, O my Son; for he that doth so, is the best and highest Philosopher; and without Philosophy, it is impossible ever to attain to the height and exactness of Piety or Religion. But he that shall learn and study the things that are, and how they are ordered and governed, and by whom, and for what cause, or to what end, will acknowledge thanks to the Workman, as to a good Father, an excellent Nurse, and a faithful Steward, and he that gives thanks shall be Pious or Religious, and he that is Religious shall know both where the truth is, and what it is, and learning that, he will be yet more and more Religious.

Knowledge moves toward gratitude the way a river moves toward the sea. To know a thing truly is to know its origin and its end. The surface is the first layer. Hermes asks for the second: what caused this, what governs it, what does it tend toward? The world is given, and giving awakens thanks.

body.caption: The macrocosm as ordered hierarchy — the world as a living diagram of divine Mind.

body.text: For never, O Son, shall or can that Soul, which while it is in the Body lightens and lifts up itself to know and comprehend that which is Good and True, slide back to the contrary: For it is infinitely enamoured thereof, and forgetteth all Evils; and when it hath learned and known its Father and Progenitor, can no more depart from that good. And let this, O Son, be the end of Religion and Piety; whereunto when thou art once arrived, thou shalt both live well, and die blessedly, whilst thy soul is not ignorant whether it must return, and fly back again. In this only, O Son, is the way to the Truth, which our Progenitors travelled in; and by which, making their journey, they at length attained to the good.

The soul that sees the Good is changed by the seeing. Truth enters through love. The soul sheds the pretence that forgetfulness is possible. Beauty is the first mercy of metaphysics — a gravid pull toward what the soul has forgotten. Love carries knowledge inward. Knowledge alone stays on the surface.

body.caption: The soul enamoured of the Good — the conjunction of seeker and source.

body.text: All things that are are moved; only that which is not is unmoveable. Every Body is changeable. Not every body is dissolveable. Some Bodies are dissolveable. Every living thing is not mortal. Not every living thing is immortal. That which may be dissolved is also corruptible. That which abides always is unchangeable. That which is unchangeable is eternal. That which is made but once, is never corrupted neither becomes any other thing. First, God; Secondly, the World; Thirdly, Man. The World for Man, Man for God. Of the Soul; that part which is Sensible is mortal, but that which is Reasonable is immortal.

Three orders nested inside one another. God, world, man. The soul stands at the seam — part sensible and mortal, part reasonable and immortal. From this seam it can descend into confusion or ascend through affinity. It can become merely reactive, or it can become musical. Ficino's art is the art of making it musical — whetting the ear to hear what order sounds like.

If the world is ordered by Mind, then colour and sound become grammar. The soul heals by learning to stand inside the pattern. Ficino's hymns and talismans are the soul re-learning its native language — a syntax of proportion and season that the cosmos speaks fluently, and the ear was made to hear.

Hermes gave Ficino a world that could be read — read like a letter from someone who knows you. The stars are signatures. The soul that learns to read them is coming home to an order it was made from. A home scored into the fabric of things before the soul arrived to find it.
